title: Crates & crates.io 🌐
description: Tapping into the massive Rust open-source ecosystem.

slides.title: What is a Crate? 📦
slides.content:
In Rust, the word **Crate** essentially means 'A Package' or 'A Library'.

Your entire project is actually a Crate. Every external piece of code you download to boost your program is also a Crate!

slides.title: crates.io 🌐
slides.content:
Just like Node.js has NPM, and Python has PyPI, Rust has **crates.io**.

It is the central registry for the entire global Rust community. Need to generate random numbers? Validate emails? Connect to AWS? There is a completely secure crate for that!

quizData.question: In the Rust ecosystem natively precisely seamlessly, what explicitly exactly is **crates.io**?

options.text: The dedicated official centralized global registry strictly successfully firmly hosting open-source strictly cleanly Rust packages successfully.
A standard cloud database natively legitimately actively truthfully

quizData.explanation: It securely efficiently natively hosts tens thoroughly legitimately thousands explicitly packages reliably cleanly exactly cleanly deeply accurately clearly safely flawlessly seamlessly strongly correctly securely completely.

slides.title: Adding Dependencies 🛒
slides.content:
To brutally add an external crate simply to your project natively efficiently securely effectively, you uniquely explicitly open your project's `Cargo.toml` file clearly entirely correctly flawlessly.

```toml
[dependencies]
rand = "0.8.5"
```
When you build, Cargo perfectly intelligently downloads the exact natively requested version powerfully clearly intelligently reliably.
